title: AppCommand::MarkRead is handled server-side but never sent from UI
description: client_task.rs has a full MarkRead handler (line 295) including retry logic and read_positions map maintenance. However, no code in main.rs (the only call site for tx.send(AppCommand::...)) ever sends MarkRead. The read-position tracking in client_task is therefore dead from the UI's perspective — load_read_positions runs at startup and the map is maintained but never used. A future maintainer adding scroll-to-read behavior will need to wire this up, but the existing server-side code gives a false impression that the feature is already complete. The dead handler also means load_read_positions() is called at startup for no observable effect. Fix: either wire MarkRead from the UI (send it when the last visible message changes on scroll) or, if that feature is deferred, add a comment clearly marking the handler as intentionally unwired and note what UI event should trigger it.
close_reason: Fixed: load_messages_for_chat returns last message ID; SelectChat and SendMessage handlers auto-mark last message as read when messages load
